faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: About making ID photos online, swapping backgrounds, and hitting file-size limits.

free.a: Yes — completely free, with no watermark and no paywall. Pixfit runs in your browser and there are no export limits.

upload.q: Will my photo be uploaded to a server?
upload.a: No. Cut-out, background replacement, cropping, and compression all happen locally in your browser. The original file never leaves your device.

background.q: How do I change the background to white / blue / red?
background.a: After uploading a photo, open the Background panel. Pixfit uses the MODNet AI model to remove the background locally, then one-tap presets switch between standard white, visa blue, ID red, or any custom hex colour.

sizes.q: Which countries and document types are supported?
sizes.a: Pixfit ships with 28 specs out of the box: Chinese national ID / 1- and 2-inch / passport, Hong Kong / Macao / Taiwan travel permits, 14 popular visa formats (US, Schengen, UK, Canada, AU/NZ, JP/KR, Southeast Asia, Russia, India), and three Chinese national exam registration formats.

compress.q: Can I compress a photo to 30 KB / 50 KB?
compress.a: Yes. In Export, the "Compress to target KB" option binary-searches JPG/WebP quality and downscales pixels if needed, landing within ±5% of your target — useful for civil-service exam portals and visa filings with strict file-size caps.

print.q: How do I lay out multiple photos onto A4 or 5R / 6R paper?
print.a: The Layout panel lets you pick paper size (A4 / 5R / 6R) and a template (eight 1-inch / sixteen 1-inch / mixed 1+2-inch, etc.). Pixfit auto-tiles the photos with crop marks and exports as PNG or vector PDF, ready for the print shop.

mobile.q: Can I use this on a phone browser?
mobile.a: Yes. Pixfit works on mobile Safari and Android Chrome. The first cut-out downloads ~6 MB of AI model weights — connect to Wi-Fi the first time, then the entire pipeline runs in-browser.

seoIntro.heading: An ID photo studio that runs inside your browser
paragraphs: Pixfit is a completely free online ID photo maker built for people who need to produce a passport photo, visa photo, national ID photo, or exam registration photo in a hurry. No downloads, no signup — open the page, upload a portrait, and you get a compliant electronic ID photo in seconds.

We use MODNet, an industry-leading portrait-segmentation AI model, running entirely in your browser to cut out the subject and replace the background. The full pipeline — cut-out, recolour, crop, compress, print layout — happens locally on your device. No photo is ever uploaded to a server.

Pixfit ships with 28 of the most common ID photo specifications: US visa (51×51mm), Schengen visa (35×45mm), UK visa (light grey background), Japan visa (45×45mm), Chinese national ID, passports for most countries, Hong Kong / Macao / Taiwan travel permits, and Chinese national exam registration formats. Every preset captures the official pixel, DPI, and file-size limits, so you can export a compliant JPG / PNG / WebP in one click.

Footer.tagline: ID photo studio inside your browser

Studio.title: Studio
Studio.subtitle: Upload a portrait to cut out, recolor the background, crop, and lay out.

seoIntro.heading: An ID photo studio inside your browser
paragraphs: The Pixfit Studio is the core editor. Upload a portrait, then step through cut-out, background replacement, cropping to spec, print layout, compression, and export — every step runs locally in your browser, with no photo ever uploaded to a server.

Supports Chinese national ID, 1- and 2-inch IDs, passports, Hong Kong / Macao / Taiwan travel permits, 14 visa formats (US, Schengen, UK, Canada, AU, NZ, JP, KR, Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand, Russia, India), and three Chinese national exam registration formats. Auto-tile onto 5R / 6R / A4 paper and export as PNG or vector PDF.

Export.dimensionsModeSpec: Sized for the selected ID-photo spec (print-ready).

Export.qualityLockedByCompress: Quality is set automatically while compressing to a target size.
Export.compressUnsupported: Compression only applies to JPG / WebP.
Export.kbHint: We binary-search quality and downscale if necessary to land in ±5% of the target.
Export.compressMiss: Closest result was {kb} KB — try a larger target or downscale further.
Export.alphaNeedsCutout: PNG with transparency needs the subject cut out first.
Export.alphaCutoutHint: Want a transparent background? Cut out the subject first.
Export.alphaGoToBackground: Open the Background tab to cut out →
Export.cropAppliedHint: Cropped to your selected spec — adjust on the Size tab if needed.
Export.downloadFailed: Couldn't produce the file — try a different format.
Export.fullResHint: Preserves original resolution — the export may take a couple of seconds.

hint.alpha: Alpha-capable formats keep the transparent background intact.
hint.flat: Flat formats bake the current background colour into the export.

detail.metaTitle: {name} photo size {w}×{h}mm · Make one free online · Pixfit
detail.metaDescription: {name} ID photo specification: {w}×{h} mm ({wpx}×{hpx} px @ {dpi} DPI), recommended background {bgLabel}. Make one in Pixfit — cut out, swap background, crop, compress, all in your browser. No upload.
detail.heading: {name} photo size
detail.intro: The standard {name} ID photo measures {w} × {h} mm ({wpx} × {hpx} px at {dpi} DPI) with a recommended background of {bgLabel}. This page covers the full specification, photography requirements, and a free in-browser tool for making one.

purpose.title: Where it's used
purpose.visa: The {name} format is required for {regionName} visa applications, including consular visas, visa-on-arrival, and eVisa portals. Embassy and consular requirements change occasionally — re-check the official guidance before you submit.
purpose.cn-id: {name} is one of the most common ID photo formats in mainland China. It's accepted for the national ID card, passport renewal, driving licence, social-security card, professional qualifications, and most workplace onboarding.
purpose.exam: {name} is the registration photo format for major Chinese national exams (civil service, postgraduate entrance, NCRE, teacher qualification). These platforms enforce strict pixel and file-size limits.
purpose.cn-paper: {name} is a common photo-lab paper size for batch-printing multiple ID photos onto a single sheet — economical when you need several wallet-sized copies.
purpose.travel-permit: {name} is required for mainland-resident travel permits to Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan, with specifications set by the Exit-Entry Administration of the Ministry of Public Security.

requirements.title: Photography & composition
requirements.body: Shoot in even lighting, facing the camera, with a neutral expression and no headwear. The head should fill about 60–75% of the frame, and shoulders shouldn't reach the bottom edge. Background should be plain ({bgLabel}) with no visible shadows. For phone selfies, use the rear-facing camera and keep the lens level with your face.

howto.subtitle: Three steps, entirely in your browser. Your photo never leaves your device.

upload.name: Upload a portrait
upload.text: Drop or pick a clear front-facing portrait. JPG / PNG / HEIC up to 50 MB per file.

cutout.name: AI background removal
cutout.text: MODNet runs locally in your browser to cut the subject out and swap the background to {bgLabel}. Switch backgrounds any time in the Background panel.

crop.name: Smart crop to {name}
crop.text: Pick the {name} preset. Pixfit finds the face and crops to {w}×{h} mm with the head centred — drag to fine-tune.

export.name: Export a file that meets the spec
export.text: Pick a format and quality. Use the size-target option if the portal requires a specific KB range, then save the file locally.

free.q: Is making a {name} photo in Pixfit free?
free.a: Yes — entirely free. Pixfit runs in your browser with no watermark, no paywall, and no export caps.

upload.q: Will my photo be uploaded to a server?
upload.a: No. Background removal, cropping, recolouring, and compression all happen locally in your browser. The original photo never leaves your device.

background.a: The recommended background is {bgLabel}. The Background panel offers one-click presets (standard white, visa blue, ID red) or any custom hex colour.

accuracy.a: Specs are compiled from public official sources. Embassies, ministries, and exam boards do update their rules — re-check the latest official guidance before submission.

phone.q: Can I make this on a phone?
phone.a: Yes. Pixfit works on mobile Safari and Chrome. The first cut-out downloads ~6 MB of AI model weights — connect to Wi-Fi the first time, then the whole pipeline runs in-browser.

privacy.heading: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: Pixfit runs entirely in your browser. We do not collect, store, or transmit your photos.
privacy.metaDescription: How Pixfit handles your data: every image stays on your device, no cookies for tracking, and the only persistent storage is your own customised specs in localStorage.

summary.title: In short
summary.body: Pixfit is a browser-only ID photo studio. Your photos never leave your device. We do not run accounts, do not require sign-up, and do not track you with cookies.

data.title: What we process
data.body: When you upload a photo, the file is decoded in your browser, processed by an on-device AI model (MODNet running on WebGPU / WebAssembly), and rendered onto a local canvas. None of those bytes are sent over the network.

localStorage.title: Local storage
localStorage.body: We use your browser's localStorage only to remember the photo / paper / layout specs you create or import (key `pixfit:specs:v1`) plus a small list of recently-used background colours. You can clear them at any time from your browser's site-settings panel.

thirdParty.title: Third-party resources
thirdParty.body: The AI model weights, fonts, and the MediaPipe runtime are fetched from public mirrors (ModelScope, jsDelivr, the Hugging Face CDN). These services see only the model file requests — never your photos. Once cached your subsequent visits are fully offline-capable for the core flow.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.body: Once we go live we plan to use Cloudflare Web Analytics, which is cookie-less and does not collect IP addresses or device fingerprints. No script is loaded until that decision ships. No third-party analytics tracker (Google / Mixpanel / Sentry) is bundled today.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: Because we do not collect personal data, there is nothing for us to delete or export on your behalf. You retain full control by clearing local site data inside your browser.

children.title: Children
children.body: Pixfit is suitable for general audiences but is not directed at children under 13. If you are using Pixfit on behalf of a minor, please review these terms with their guardian.

changes.title: Updates to this policy
changes.body: If our data-handling practices ever change we will update this page and bump the last-updated date. Material changes will also be summarised on the home page or via the in-app notification banner.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Open an issue at github.com/aapw01/id-photo-tool or email the maintainer listed in the repository README.

terms.heading: Terms of service
terms.subtitle: Pixfit is offered free of charge, as-is, and with no warranties. By using it you agree to these terms.
terms.metaDescription: Pixfit is free and open source. You agree to use it only with photos you have the right to process, and accept the software is provided as-is.

scope.title: Scope
scope.body: These terms cover any use of pix-fit.com and the open-source Pixfit code base. By loading the site or running the code you agree to be bound by them.

service.title: What we provide
service.body: A browser-based ID photo studio. Every feature runs locally; the host service exists only to serve static files. We do not promise any specific level of availability or accuracy.

responsibility.title: Your responsibility
responsibility.body: You must hold the rights to every photo you process. Do not upload other people's photos without their consent and do not use Pixfit to violate any government photo standard, identity-verification policy, or local law.

compliance.title: Spec accuracy
compliance.body: Built-in photo specifications are cross-referenced from official sources at the time of writing. Embassies and exam boards change their rules regularly — please double-check the latest official requirement before submitting your application.

ip.title: Intellectual property
ip.body: The Pixfit source code is released under the MIT license. Built-in icons, fonts, and the AI model are licensed by their respective authors (see the README). Your photos remain yours.

warranty.title: No warranty
warranty.body: The service is provided as-is and as-available, without warranties of any kind, express or implied. We disclaim warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of Pixfit, including but not limited to rejected applications or printing costs.

changes.title: Changes to the service
changes.body: We may add, change, or remove features at any time. Material changes will be announced through the changelog in the open-source repository.

law.title: Governing law
law.body: Disputes are governed by the laws of the jurisdiction where the maintainer resides, and where applicable, by the relevant local consumer-protection law.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Open an issue at github.com/aapw01/id-photo-tool for any term-related question.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you are looking for has been moved or never existed. Head back to the studio or the home page to keep going.

runtime.title: Something went wrong
runtime.body: Pixfit hit an unexpected error rendering this page. The issue has been logged in your browser console. Try retrying or reload the page; if it persists, please open an issue.

Scanner.title: ID Document Scanner
Scanner.subtitle: Upload phone-shot photos of your ID card, passport, or driver license and instantly generate an official-looking PDF scan or photocopy — fully in your browser, no upload, no signup.
Scanner.metaDescription: Free browser-based ID document scanner: upload an ID card, passport, or driver license photo and get an A4 PDF with perspective correction and anti-abuse watermark. Scan / photocopy / enhance modes. 100% offline processing.

shell.comingSoonHeading: ID Document Scanner is on the way
shell.comingSoonBody: We're polishing perspective correction, scan / photocopy rendering, mandatory watermark, and A4 layout. The shell is here today; features roll out in upcoming iterations.

editor.title: Drag the four handles to the document edges
editor.hint: Magnify the corners with pinch or scroll to land them precisely. Keyboard: Tab between handles, arrow keys nudge ±1 px, Shift+arrow ±10 px.

config.modeHint: Switching re-colors the rectified result; no need to redetect

watermark.label: Anti-misuse watermark
watermark.mandatoryNote: Watermark is mandatory and clamped to a 30 % minimum opacity (compliance requirement)

export.noContent: Upload at least one side to enable export

history.title: Recent sessions
history.empty: Your recent settings appear here after the first export
history.hint: Last {max} configurations · settings only, no images stored

usage.heading: Before you scan — usage notice
usage.summary: Read this before you upload your ID. Your photos never leave the browser — this is what we do, what we don't, and what you agree to by using the scanner.

local.title: Everything stays on your device
local.body: Perspective correction, photocopy / scan / enhance rendering, watermark, and PDF assembly all run locally in your browser. We do not host, upload, or analyse your document photos.

watermark.title: Watermark is mandatory
watermark.body: Every exported PDF / PNG carries an anti-misuse watermark. You customise the text (e.g. "For XYZ Co. onboarding only") and opacity (minimum 30 %), but the watermark itself can not be disabled. This is a compliance requirement to deter ID-card impersonation.

lawful.title: Lawful use only
lawful.body: Use Pixfit Scanner only with documents you have the right to scan and only for purposes permitted by law in your jurisdiction. Forging, altering, or impersonating identity documents is prohibited.

accuracy.title: Submission is your responsibility
accuracy.body: We do our best to match flatbed-scanner output, but official portals may have stricter spec on DPI, color, file size, or formatting. Verify the destination portal's requirements before submitting and re-export if needed.

paragraphs.0: Job applications, online filings, visa submissions, and contract signing often require a 'scanned' PDF — not a phone snapshot. But most people don't own a scanner. The ID Document Scanner rectifies, white-balances, and shadow-strips your phone-shot ID photo to mimic real scanner output, and exports it as A4 PDF with one click.
paragraphs.1: Photocopy mode applies adaptive binarization for the black-and-white look that exam registrations and government offices expect. Every output carries a watermark that cannot be fully disabled — you choose the text (e.g. 'For XYZ Company onboarding only') to deter ID-card misuse and impersonation fraud. The entire pipeline runs in your browser; nothing is ever uploaded.

supported.heading: Supported document types
supported.subtitle: Eleven built-in document profiles cover the most common scan targets across China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Singapore, India, the US, plus generic passport bio pages and full-page A4 / Letter documents.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: About scanning documents in your browser, anti-misuse watermarks, and output accuracy.

free.q: Is the ID Document Scanner free?
free.a: Yes — completely free, no signup, no usage limits. The whole pipeline runs in your browser, so we have no server cost to pass on.

privacy.q: Will my document photo be uploaded to a server?
privacy.a: No. Perspective correction, white balance, photocopy mode, watermark, and PDF assembly all happen locally on your device. Nothing is sent over the network.

watermark.a: Photo-based ID-document fraud is a real abuse vector. The watermark text (which you customise, e.g. 'For XYZ Company onboarding only') is clamped to a minimum 30 % opacity so the output can't easily be cleaned up and forwarded. This is a compliance requirement we do not offer to disable.

official.q: Is the output accepted by government portals?
official.a: Most online submission portals accept rectified phone-camera scans, and our output mimics a flatbed scanner closely. That said, official requirements differ — for high-stakes filings (visas, banking) we recommend reading the portal's exact spec before submitting.

accuracy.q: How accurate is the auto perspective correction?
accuracy.a: OpenCV.js detects the four document corners from the largest enclosed quadrilateral. Most well-lit, contrast-clean phone photos resolve in under 200 ms. If detection misses, you can drag the four corner handles manually — keyboard arrows + Shift work too.

detail.metaDescription: Free in-browser scanner for {name}: upload a phone-shot photo, get an A4 / Letter / A5 PDF with perspective correction, anti-misuse watermark, and scan / photocopy / enhance output modes. 100 % offline.
detail.tagline: {w} × {h} mm · A4 / Letter / A5 PDF · Mandatory watermark

summarySection.title: About {name}
summarySection.subtitle: What it is and where it gets requested

specSection.title: Physical specification
specSection.twoSidedYes: Yes — front + back are packed onto the same sheet

howTo.title: How to scan {name} with Pixfit
howTo.subtitle: Four steps, entirely in your browser. Your photo never leaves your device.

upload.name: Upload a phone-shot photo of {name}
upload.text: Drop the front (and optionally the back) photo into the scanner workspace. JPG / PNG / HEIC up to 20 MB per file. We decode EXIF orientation automatically.

detect.name: Auto perspective correction
detect.text: OpenCV.js detects the four corners of {name} and warps the image to a flat scanner-style rectangle. If a corner is off, drag the handle — keyboard arrows work too.

configure.name: Pick output mode + watermark
configure.text: Switch between Scan (color + auto white balance), Copy (B&W binarize), or Enhance (saturation + contrast). Customise the mandatory anti-misuse watermark text + opacity + density.

export.name: Export as A4 / Letter / A5 PDF
export.text: Pack both sides onto a single sheet with cut marks, and download as PDF or PNG. The file is generated locally — no upload required.

free.a: Yes. Pixfit's ID Document Scanner is free with no watermark on the workspace, no signup, and no monthly cap. The output PDF carries an anti-misuse watermark, but that's by design (and you customise the text).

privacy.q: Will the {name} photo be uploaded to a server?
privacy.a: No. Perspective correction, watermark compositing, and PDF assembly all run locally in your browser. The phone-shot photo of {name} never leaves your device.

watermark.q: Can I remove the watermark when scanning {name}?
watermark.a: The text and opacity are customisable (down to 30 %) but the watermark itself can not be disabled. This is an anti-fraud measure: an ID document photo with no anti-misuse mark is too easy to redirect into impersonation scams.

official.q: Will the scan of {name} be accepted by official portals?
official.a: Most online submission portals accept rectified phone-camera scans of {name}, and the output mimics a flatbed scanner closely. For high-stakes filings, check the destination portal's exact spec — file size, DPI, color or black-and-white — and pick the matching output mode before exporting.

cn-id-card.summary: The PRC Resident Identity Card (居民身份证) is the standard CR80-sized (85.6 × 54 mm) two-sided ID issued to all Chinese citizens.
cn-id-card.usage: Workplace onboarding, hotel check-in, banking, exam registrations, courier verification, and online portal sign-ups across mainland China. Most online forms request both the portrait side and the national-emblem side on a single page.

hk-id-card.summary: The Hong Kong Identity Card (香港身份證) is the standard CR80-sized (85.6 × 54 mm) identity card issued to Hong Kong residents.
hk-id-card.usage: Banking, telecom contracts, employment paperwork, and travel scenarios where Hong Kong residents must produce a scanned identity card. The smart-chip front and the bilingual back are routinely scanned together.

tw-id-card.summary: The Taiwan National Identity Card (中華民國身分證) is the standard CR80-sized (85.6 × 54 mm) two-sided national ID issued in the Republic of China.
tw-id-card.usage: Banking, government services, postal pickup, telecom contracts, and online portal sign-ups across Taiwan. Front (national emblem + photo) and back (parental info) are usually scanned together.

sg-nric.summary: The Singapore National Registration Identity Card (NRIC) is the standard CR80-sized (85.6 × 54 mm) two-sided ID issued to Singapore citizens and permanent residents.
sg-nric.usage: Bank account opening, telecom signups, HDB applications, healthcare registration, and corporate onboarding paperwork. The card's front (photo + NRIC number) and back (residential address) are often scanned together.

in-aadhaar.summary: The Indian Aadhaar card is a CR80-sized (85.6 × 54 mm) two-sided national identity proof issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI).
in-aadhaar.usage: KYC for banks, SIM cards, government welfare schemes, tax filings, and many private-sector services across India. Both the photo side and the QR-code side are commonly scanned for submission.

us-driver-license.summary: US state driver licenses are CR80-sized (85.6 × 54 mm) two-sided plastic cards issued by each state's Department of Motor Vehicles.
us-driver-license.usage: I-9 employment eligibility, lease applications, rental cars, alcohol purchase, TSA travel verification, and identity proofing for online services. Many employers and landlords ask for both sides on a single PDF.

passport-bio.summary: Passport bio pages are ISO/IEC 7810 ID-3 sized (125 × 88 mm) and contain the photograph, machine-readable zone, and personal details.
passport-bio.usage: Visa applications, hotel check-in, corporate onboarding for foreign hires, bank KYC, and any scenario that requires identity proof for non-resident travel. Only the bio page is scanned; the rest of the passport stays private.

cn-driver-license.summary: The PRC driver license (机动车驾驶证) is an 88 × 60 mm booklet card with photo and personal info on the front and endorsement classes on the back.
cn-driver-license.usage: Car rental, rideshare driver registration, traffic court paperwork, insurance claims, and employer-mandated driving privilege verification.

cn-vehicle-license.summary: The PRC vehicle license (行驶证) is an 88 × 60 mm booklet card listing the vehicle's plate number, owner, model, and engine ID.
cn-vehicle-license.usage: Insurance renewal, rideshare onboarding (Didi etc.), car resale, parking permit applications, and any traffic-law-related filing where vehicle ownership must be proven.

a4.summary: A4 (210 × 297 mm) is the ISO 216 standard letter-size paper used outside North America.
a4.usage: Single-page contracts, receipts, certificates, transcripts, and any business document the user needs to fold-flat and 'scan' from a phone photo into a clean PDF.

letter.summary: US Letter (215.9 × 279.4 mm) is the North American standard letter-size paper — slightly wider and shorter than A4.
letter.usage: Single-page US contracts, IRS forms, US-printed receipts, and any North American business document the user needs to scan into a clean PDF from a phone photo.
